last_completed_round.direction_name: Retune The Active PTX One-K 128x128 Hot-Band Control Path
last_completed_round.hypothesis: Round 2/20 falsified the 256x128 geometry pivot hard: end-to-end runtime regressed from the accepted 24.419329 ms PTX base to 30.286848 ms, and the hot-band kernel alone grew to 43.291456 ms even though registers fell to 168/thread. That means the best next move is not another wide family jump but a bounded exploit pass on top of the accepted PTX one-k 128x128 branch. The winning PTX base still had 200 registers per thread, only 16.62% active warps, 7.20% long-scoreboard stall, and 5.41% barrier stall, so there is still room to trim local control/export overhead without repeating the failed geometry experiment.
